posts.author: Taiga
posts.content:
[Predictions]

Boldbaatar vs. Suzuki
Takaya Suzuki, DEC
This is a really good prospect fight and might be the best prospect vs prospect fight in this whole tournament, its a shame one of them has to be eliminated so early. Takaya Suzuki is one of the best flyweight prospects in the world currently and has improved tremendously over the past year sense he got that 4 second viral knockout. Suzuki has been training at Killcliff and has been living in America for over a year now getting reps with some of the best fighters in the world. I think this fight will be mainly standing but it wouldn't see surprise me too see Boldbaatar mix in grappling but Suzuki's takedown defense and grappling has improved tremendously due to training with Mitch McKee, Nikita Kulshin, and Ansar Khamzaev. He has really nice footwork and he's very fast, able to prevent shots from reaching due to that footwork and speed, rarely being flat footed making him quite elusive. Suzuki carries power that is rare for flyweight and we saw that when he starched a very solid prospect in Figueiredo at Fury, he sets up his shots with nice feints and his counters are lethal, his hands are extremely fast even for flyweights. Boldaatar is a very aggressive athletic fighter who has legitimate power much like Suzuki, he has nice wheel kicks that he uses often, along with knees and leg kicks. He often crashes in and unleashes lots of strikes at once and he can often catch his opponent during these exchanges but also leaving him vulnerable, his defense isn't the greatest. When his opponent is hurt he doesn't stop with the pressure and will very aggressively seek the finish. Suzuki will have the cleaner striking of the two but Boldaatar aggression might cause Suzuki some problems, I think Suzuki will outpoint Boldaatar to a decision but Suzuki catching Boldaatar as he crashes in wouldn't surprise me, on the other hand Boldaatar overwhelming Suzuki early and finding the finish would also not surprise me. Boldaatar had to pull out of a recent Shooto fight where he would of faced Fujita due to a knee injury. Its unknown if that injury was legit or if he will be fighting with this injury or that's its healed. If the rumors of a ACL tear is true than Boldaatar will be fighting on it, think this fight swings in Suzuki's favor tremendously. Think this is a 60/40 fight even without the Boldaatar Injury rumors and if Suzuki can overcome this tough challenge than this tournament is his too lose.

* Edited at 04.01.2026,  2:06 PM ET *
